content:
In-Person Tutoring Rates in South Africa 2026

How much does a private tutor charge in South Africa? In-person tutoring rates vary significantly based on the subject, the tutor's experience, grade level, and your location. This guide covers the current 2026 tutoring rates across South Africa so you can budget effectively and find the best value for your child's education.

Average In-Person Tutoring Rates 2026

Tutor TypeHourly Rate (ZAR)Notes
University student tutorR150–R250/hourGood for Grade 8–10, basic subjects
Qualified teacher (private)R250–R400/hourCAPS curriculum expert
Subject specialist (experienced)R350–R600/hourGrade 11–12, Maths/Science focus
Matric specialistR400–R600/hourNSC exam prep, IEB tutor
Group tutoring (3–5 students)R100–R200/student/hourCost-effective option
Tutoring centre (franchise)R200–R450/hourStructured programmes, fixed schedule

Tutoring Rates by Subject (2026)

SubjectTypical Hourly RateWhy It Costs This Much
Mathematics (Grade 10–12)R300–R600/hourHigh demand, specialist knowledge required
Physical Sciences (Grade 10–12)R300–R550/hourCombined Physics & Chemistry, specialist knowledge
Accounting (Grade 10–12)R280–R500/hourTechnical subject, strong demand
English (Grade 8–12)R200–R400/hourLanguage skills, essay writing
Life Sciences (Grade 10–12)R250–R450/hourModerate demand
Economics (Grade 10–12)R250–R450/hourModerate demand
Mathematics Grade 8–9R200–R350/hourFoundation level, less specialist
History / GeographyR200–R350/hourLower specialist threshold

Tutoring Rates by Region (2026)

RegionTypical Rate RangeNotes
Johannesburg / Sandton / MidrandR350–R600/hourHighest rates in SA
Cape Town (Southern Suburbs, Atlantic Seaboard)R300–R550/hourHigh demand, affluent areas
PretoriaR300–R500/hourSimilar to Johannesburg suburbs
Durban / UmhlangaR250–R450/hourSlightly lower than Joburg
Port Elizabeth / GqeberhaR200–R400/hourMid-range rates
Smaller cities / townsR150–R300/hourLower cost of living
Rural areasR100–R250/hourFewer tutors available, lower rates

Monthly Cost of In-Person Tutoring

At 2 sessions of 1.5 hours per week, here is what in-person tutoring costs annually:

Hourly RatePer Session (1.5h)Per Week (×2)Per Month (×8)Per Year (×40 weeks)
R250/hourR375R750R3,000R15,000
R350/hourR525R1,050R4,200R21,000
R450/hourR675R1,350R5,400R27,000
R600/hourR900R1,800R7,200R36,000

When you add transport costs (fuel or taxi fare to and from tutoring sessions), the total cost of in-person tutoring can easily reach R20,000–R40,000 per year.

What Affects In-Person Tutoring Rates?

  • Grade level: Grade 12 / matric tutoring costs more than lower grades because it requires a more specialised, experienced tutor
  • Subject: Mathematics, Physical Sciences, and Accounting tutors typically charge more than Language and Social Sciences tutors
  • Tutor qualifications: A qualified teacher with 10+ years' experience charges more than a university student
  • Location: Gauteng and Western Cape rates are higher than other provinces
  • Session frequency: Some tutors offer discounts for booking 3+ sessions per week
  • Travel: Tutors who travel to your home charge a travel premium

Tips for Getting the Best Value from a Tutor

  • Be specific about your needs: Know exactly which topics your child is struggling with to avoid wasting expensive session time
  • Prepare before each session: Have questions ready, do your homework — don't use expensive tutor time on things you can figure out yourself
  • Consider group sessions: Group tutoring with 2–4 friends can reduce the cost per student by 50–60%
  • Use AI tutoring for daily practice: Tools like StudyBuddy cost R99/month for unlimited tutoring — use it for daily homework and reserve the human tutor for complex topics

Compare: In-Person Tutoring vs AI Tutoring

FeatureIn-Person TutorStudyBuddy AI Tutor
CostR200–R600/hourFREE to start / R99/month unlimited
Availability2–3 sessions per week24/7 — anytime, any topic
Subjects coveredUsually 1–2 subjectsAll CAPS subjects (Grades 8–12)
ConsistencyVaries by tutorAlways consistent quality
TransportTravel requiredNo travel — study from home
Exam prepGoodExcellent (with past papers access)

For most South African families, the most cost-effective approach is to use StudyBuddy AI tutoring (FREE to start) for daily homework and exam practice, with occasional in-person tutoring for specific complex topics.
keywords: how much does a tutor cost
quickAnswer: In-person tutoring in South Africa costs R150–R600 per hour in 2026, depending on the subject, grade, tutor's experience, and your location. Johannesburg rates tend to be highest (R350–R600/hour), while smaller towns are lower (R150–R300/hour).

faqs.question: How much does an in-person tutor cost in South Africa in 2026?
faqs.answer: In-person tutoring typically costs R150–R600 per hour in South Africa. University student tutors charge R150–R250/hour, while experienced subject specialists charge R350–R600/hour. Rates are highest in Johannesburg and Cape Town.

faqs.question: How much does a Grade 12 maths tutor cost per hour?
faqs.answer: A Grade 12 Mathematics tutor typically charges R300–R600 per hour in South Africa, depending on the tutor's qualifications and your location. Experienced matric maths tutors in Johannesburg can charge up to R600/hour.

faqs.question: Is there a cheaper alternative to in-person tutoring?
faqs.answer: Yes. AI tutoring tools like StudyBuddy offer unlimited tutoring in all subjects for just R99/month — compared to R3,000–R7,000 per month for regular in-person sessions. Many families use AI tutoring for daily homework and reserve human tutors for specific complex topics.

faqs.question: Are online tutors cheaper than in-person tutors?
faqs.answer: Yes, online tutors typically charge 10–20% less than in-person tutors as there are no travel costs. Online human tutors typically charge R200–R450/hour. AI tutoring is even more affordable at a flat R99/month for unlimited help.
